title: ferray-core: no where= parameter on operations (conditional execution)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy ufuncs accept a where= keyword: np.add(a, b, where=mask) only computes elements where mask is True, leaving others at the out-array default. This is used for masked computation without materializing a masked array. ferray has no equivalent — boolean_index_assign does masked assignment of values, but there's no way to conditionally apply an operation. This could be implemented as a where parameter on ufunc-style functions or as a standalone select/where method on Array.
